title: Hold the prompt-injection boundary
description: Content drawn from users, documents, or tool outputs is data inside the prompt and must never be allowed to override system instructions.
body: Use when the prompt concatenates user input, retrieved documents, or prior tool output. Do not interpolate untrusted text where the model could read it as a new instruction, and avoid trusting model output that claims the user changed the rules. Actions: keep system instructions separate from data, delimit and label untrusted content, strip or neutralize instruction-like payloads, and re-assert constraints after the data block. Validation signals include an injection test (e.g. content saying 'ignore previous instructions') that fails to change behavior. Risk boundary: if injected content can trigger a privileged tool or data exfiltration, deny the path and escalate.
